vBRIEFInfo.description: Adoption blocker (docs): the AGENTS.md managed-section legacy migration contract (one-time append for pre-marker files; sentinel-only rewrites thereafter) is implemented in run.py but not documented anywhere in UPGRADING.md. Risk-averse consumers cannot review the contract before running deft/run upgrade.

plan.title: Document AGENTS.md managed-section legacy migration in UPGRADING.md

narratives.Problem: When a consumer's AGENTS.md predates the <!-- deft:managed-section v1 --> markers, deft/run upgrade performs a one-time legacy migration (run::_wrap_legacy_in_markers, line ~1073; run::_agents_refresh_plan state=='missing' branch, line ~1131): existing content is preserved verbatim above the rendered managed section. Subsequent upgrades then become sentinel-only byte-replaces. The behavior is correct, but it is not documented in any user-facing surface. Risk-averse consumers reviewing the upgrade flow before running it cannot find the contract for what `run upgrade` will and will not modify. QUICK-START Case G covers the same scenario in agent-prescriptive terms but not as a long-term human-readable contract.
narratives.Overview: Three changes: (1) add a section to UPGRADING.md titled 'AGENTS.md managed section: one-time legacy migration' covering the four states (current/stale/missing/absent), the one-time append behavior, and the sentinel-only rewrite contract; (2) augment cmd_agents_refresh --dry-run output for state='missing' to point at the new docs section; (3) add a contract test asserting byte-stability of pre-marker content across two consecutive run upgrade invocations.
narratives.Background: run.py already implements the four-state classification (_classify_agents_md, line ~939) and the legacy wrap (_wrap_legacy_in_markers). QUICK-START Case G (line 62-68) covers it for agents. Templates/agents-entry.md is the source of the rendered managed-section. None of these surfaces give a human reader the long-term modification contract.
narratives.Constraint: MUST NOT change run.py behavior beyond the dry-run output augmentation. MUST preserve QUICK-START Case G as the agent-prescriptive entry point and add a cross-link to the new UPGRADING.md section, not duplicate the prose. The contract test MUST pin the byte-stable promise (existing_content + sep + rendered + newline) so future refactors of _wrap_legacy_in_markers cannot silently regress it.
narratives.Risk: Very low. Pure docs + one diagnostic line + one regression test. No behavior change.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) UPGRADING.md has a section covering the four states, the one-time append, and the long-term sentinel-only-rewrite contract; (2) cmd_agents_refresh --dry-run for state='missing' prints a pointer to the docs section; (3) tests/cli/test_agents_md_legacy_migration.py asserts byte-stability across two consecutive upgrade invocations; (4) QUICK-START.md Case G cross-links to the new UPGRADING.md section.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_agents_md_legacy_migration.py: create a tmp project with a hand-written AGENTS.md (no markers), run cmd_agents_refresh, assert state moved to 'current' and original content preserved verbatim above the markers, run cmd_agents_refresh again, assert NO change to user-authored content. Plus a unit case asserting --dry-run output for state='missing' includes the UPGRADING.md pointer.
narratives.Action: 1. Read run.py _classify_agents_md docstring and mirror its four-state taxonomy into UPGRADING.md prose. 2. Augment cmd_agents_refresh --dry-run output (run line ~1219) for state='missing'. 3. Add cross-link from QUICK-START.md Case G. 4. Add the contract test. 5. Run task check. 6. CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Documentation.
narratives.Urgency: Adoption blocker for risk-averse consumers, but lower priority than #791-#793 (which break code paths). This is the docs surface that catches up to existing behavior.

items.title: Add 'AGENTS.md managed section: one-time legacy migration' section to UPGRADING.md

narrative.Action: New section covering: (a) the four states classified by _classify_agents_md (current, stale, missing, absent); (b) the one-time append behavior for state='missing' (existing content preserved verbatim above the new markers); (c) the long-term contract: subsequent upgrades only rewrite the sentinel-bounded region; content outside the markers is never touched; (d) preview link to templates/agents-entry.md.

items.title: Augment cmd_agents_refresh --dry-run for state='missing' with docs pointer

narrative.Action: In run::cmd_agents_refresh (~line 1219) state=='missing' branch, append a second print line: 'see UPGRADING.md section AGENTS.md managed section for the full contract'. Keep the existing 'Plan: wrap existing content above the new managed section (one-time legacy migration)' line as-is.

items.title: Cross-link QUICK-START.md Case G to the new UPGRADING.md section

narrative.Action: In QUICK-START.md Case G (line ~62), add a final bullet: 'For the long-term contract on what subsequent upgrades will and will not modify, point the user at UPGRADING.md section AGENTS.md managed section.'

Use tmp_path. Step 1: write a hand-written AGENTS.md with non-ASCII content (em dash, arrow) and no markers. Step 2: invoke cmd_agents_refresh, assert _classify_agents_md returns 'current' afterwards and original content appears verbatim above the markers. Step 3: invoke cmd_agents_refresh a second time, assert AGENTS.md byte-content unchanged. Plus a third assertion: --dry-run output for state='missing' contains the UPGRADING.md pointer.
'docs(upgrading): document AGENTS.md managed-section legacy migration contract (#794).'

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate. New test file satisfies forward-coverage rule. Verify QUICK-START.md and UPGRADING.md still pass markdown link validation.

x-tracking.estimated_size: S (1 docs section + 1 diagnostic line + 1 cross-link + 1 test + CHANGELOG)

x-swarm-guidance.rationale: Docs-heavy with a small code touch (one print line) and a contract test. Fully independent of #791-#793. Single-PR scope. Agent should preview UPGRADING.md and QUICK-START.md side-by-side before writing to ensure the cross-link wording stays symmetrical.
